key_points: The Baudhāyana-Pythagoras theorem applies only to right-angled triangles
The hypotenuse is always the longest side of a right triangle
Squares constructed on the sides of a right triangle help illustrate the theorem
Pythagorean triples are sets of integers satisfying a² + b² = c²
Primitive triples have no common factors except 1
examples: Rectangle diagonal example: sides 9 and 12 → diagonal 15
exam_tips: Square the sides before comparing values
Always check if the largest side is used as the hypotenuse
Use known Pythagorean triples to simplify calculations
